Executive Summary
• 	Now we’re going to talk about some of the tools that we use in our launches.
These are tools that we use and recommend.
• 	I’m going to cover launch pages, funnel pages, webpages, video pages, and all
those types of things. How do you actually build the launch that everyone is
going to see?
• 	First of all, the great thing is that these tools just keep on getting better. If you
don't see something on this list or in the Launch Resource Toolkit (Bonus 4),
it doesn't mean it doesn't work; it just means that I don't have experience
with it.
• 	“Horses for courses” - this idea is that in horse racing certain horses are
better for certain courses, compared to other courses - There are different
horses for different courses, and there are different tools that are better for
different situations. Just because you see me using a specific tool doesn't
mean that’s the one that you should automatically be using.
Tools:
• 	Leadpages (http://productlaunchformula.com/leadpages) - I absolutely love
Leadpages. This is how we create pretty much all the pages for our business
right now. It's great for squeeze pages, sales pages, webinar pages, webcast
pages, thank you pages, etc. They keep on adding more pages, and they’re
always testing designs.
• 	Kajabi (http://productlaunchformula.com/kajabibp) - We’ve used Kajabi -
they have a great service. It's amazing for online video courses.
• 	OptimizePress (http://productlaunchformula.com/optimizepress) - I’ve
never used this one myself. My team might have used it for some pages, but I
know a lot people really like it. It's good for squeeze pages and there's launch
funnel pages too.
• 	WordPress (http://productlaunchformula.com/wordpress) - a great, free,
comprehensive Content Management System for building websites. It's a
great place to start. That's what my blog at JeffWalker.com is built on.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
Resources- Tools, Part 1

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Transcript
Ok – so these are the tools that we use to build out launches. They’re the
launch pages, funnel pages, webpages, video pages, etc. These are the tools that we
use and we recommend. Some of these tools, we’re only going to use one of the tools
in the category, but there might be lots of great tools. We’ll give you some
alternatives.
These aren’t all going to be things that we’ve used in personal experience but
because we have this platinum plus mastermind and because we have our launch
club community where we’re getting just constant feedback on what’s working and
what’s not working, we’ll give you some recommendations of things that we’ve heard
good stuff about from people that we trust. These aren’t all going to be 100% from
personal use, but they’re either going to be from personal use or from
recommendations from people that we know, like and trust.
In general, the great thing is these tools are better than they’ve ever been.
When I started out, I was hacking together the ugliest pages you can ever imagine.
You got to remember I’m not a designer. I’m not a coder. I don't know HTML. I don't
know anything like that. I would just build these horrible, ugly pages. Now you can
build beautiful pages that are super effective pages. Just the level of tools is just
absolutely amazing. You can create just like fill out a few forms and dragging and
dropping. It’s absolutely amazing. They keep on getting better.
One of these things is these are now what they call SASes, software as a
service. In other words, for almost all of these, you’ll pay on a monthly basis. On the
one hand, that’s not great because you're paying on a monthly basis. On the other
hand, they get better every month. They keep on getting better and better and better
and they keep on improving them and they have lots of people using them and they
got lots of feedback from the people. It really is a fantastic evolution where the world
has gone in terms of these kinds of tools.
One thing is- this list is not comprehensive. There’s always new tools coming
out, new software coming out - and we can’t keep up with them. This isn’t designed
to be a consumer guide to software. I’m just telling you some of the things that work
and some of the things that we use, some of the things that some of our closest clients
use.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

TRANSCRIPT
Resources- Tools, Part 1
4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
There’s this phrase, horses for courses. I don't know if this is an
Australian term or not, but I first heard it from my Aussie friends. It’s this idea that
in a horse race, there are some horses that are good in some courses and that are not
so good in other courses. I like boats. There are some boats, you might want a boat
for cruising around the lake with a few friends. Another one you might want for
crossing the Atlantic Ocean, and another boat you’d want for paddling down a white
water river, and they are radically different boats because they’re designed for
different purposes. Different tools are designed for different purposes.
Also, if you see me using a tool, it doesn’t mean it’s the right tool
for you. I spent the first 10 years of my business alone, just me, and then as my wife
helping me with some of the customer service and some of the finance stuff, but it
was just me. I was limited in what tools I could use. I started to grow my team
gradually. Now it’s a significant size team and I’ve got all kinds of capabilities. We use
tools that if I was doing it myself, they’d probably be too complicated for me. They
certainly would have been way too expensive for me back when I started out. Just
because you see me using something, it doesn’t mean it’s the right choice for you. It
might be, but it might not be. People are always interested in what we’re using. I’ll
tell you what we’re using, but just because we’re using it, it doesn’t mean it’s the right
one for you.
The other thing is that all these tools have their own tutorials.
That’s a great thing because they’re a lot better at making tutorials than I am. We
don’t have tutorials for these various tools because they all have their own tutorials
and they’re all professionals at it and they’re all really good at it and they’re all
continuing and updating those tools or those tutorials. There’s no way I could
possibly compete with their tutorials.
About these recommendations, some of them are going to be affiliate links,
and I’ll get a small commission if you follow my link. You're welcome to not follow
my link. You can find it all on Google I’m sure. The one thing is I’m only going to
recommend something that I really believe in because it’ll be insane for me to risk my
relationship with you by telling you to go use a tool that’s inferior or I don’t believe
in. The reality is all these affiliate links make up … If I added up all the commissions I
make from all these links, it’s not going to make a significant difference in my
business. It wouldn’t be a drop in the bucket.
With no further ado, here are some of the stuff we’re using or we like.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Resources- Tools, Part 1
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Leadpages, big fan of Leadpages. We use them to create our funnels, our
launches. They’re great for opt-in pages, sales page, webinar pages, webcast pages,
thank you pages, on and on and on. This last launch, we actually used custom pages.
We started with Leadpages designs and then we customized. This is the first year we
ever did full out where I had a designer making custom pages for me. Up until now,
last year, we’ve been using Leadpages. I’m really happy with them.
A big disclaimer, it’s run by a friend of mine, Clay Collins, a longtime friend.
I’m talking … He was in Minnesota. He flew out. He came to one of a surprise
birthday party my wife gave for me a couple of years ago. He flew out from
Minnesota up to my home in Colorado and attended my surprise birthday party. He’s
a good friend, known him for a very, very long time. One of the things is Clay … He’s
got a big team now. It’s a big team. It’s a big organization. It’s a marketing-driven
organization. These designs are tested. They’re tested in that they’re good marketing
designs.
The thing is they’re able to look at all their users and all the pages their users
are using and they get to see what converts and what doesn’t convert. They’re always
coming up with new pages and testing them and making sure they create pages that
convert. That’s one of the reasons I love Leadpages. They do integrate with
WordPress, integrate with Facebook. You can create standalone pages or they’ll
actually host the page for you. A big, big fan of Leadpages. You can get there,
productlaunchformula.com/leadpages.
Kajabi is something that we used for many years. It’s great for building
launch funnels and opt-in pages and squeeze pages. Also, it’s great for hosting online
video courses. I think we used Kajabi for about four years to host product launch
formula. We’re long-term fans of Kajabi. We finally left Kajabi when we wanted to
build out really a custom portal for product launch formula to really go super custom
with it. Otherwise, we were really happy using Kajabi for many years. They’re good
friends of ours. We have people in launch club and people in platinum plus that are
using … One person in platinum plus, he tends to be really hard on software, really
use it to its full extent. He’s been really happy with the new Kajabi. Big fans,
productlaunchformula.com/kajabibp will get you there.
OptimizePress is another one. I’ve never used this one myself but I do know
a lot of people that are happy with OptimizePress.
Clickfunnels, this is another one we haven’t used but I’m super impressed
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
